tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship is high-priority with a tight deadline and requires focused effort, while the fun option has no consequences if skipped. I should prioritize completing the application to secure funding.
Starting the scholarship application now builds a safety buffer against delays, reducing future stress without sacrificing any fun opportunities later.
Despite 1.5 hours of work, the scholarship application is only 37% complete with a tight deadline, leaving significant pressure due to my current sleep debt and stress levels.
I made modest progress on the scholarship but missed a casual fun option, which is acceptable given the urgent deadline and my current stress levels.
After 1.5 hours, the scholarship application is only 57% complete with three days left, meaning my current pace is insufficient given my sleep debt and stress.
The scholarship application is now 63% complete after another hour of work, but the remaining time and my current stress levels still make the three-day deadline challenging.
After 1.5 hours, the scholarship application is 69% complete, but my low energy and high stress mean I still need to work efficiently to finish in three days.
My scholarship application is now 75% complete after another 1.5 hours, yet my energy remains at zero and stress is high, making the final push critical.
The scholarship application is now 81% complete after 1.5 hours, but my energy is depleted and stress remains high with only three days left.
After only 1.5 hours of sleep, my energy remains critically low at 1.8 with high stress, yet the scholarship application is nearing completion.
The scholarship application is now 89% complete after two hours of work, though my energy remains low and stress high with three days left.
The scholarship application is now 93% complete after another hour of work, though my energy remains critically low and stress high.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
